script_text: The word said is tricky. How is it spelled?
Four letters: s-a-i-d. But it sounds like "sed"!
Which tricky word means "in the past"?
Yesterday I was happy. Was = past!
How is the tricky word have spelled?
Have ends with a silent E!
Which tricky word means "more than one person was"?
We were here yesterday. Were = past for many!
Why are words like said and have called tricky?
Tricky words don't follow the usual sound rules. You just memorize them!
